formulas.name: Malus' Law
formulas.variables: I: Intensity of light passing through the polarizer, I0: Initial intensity of light, θ: Angle between polarizer and analyzer
formulas.units: Intensity (I) and Initial Intensity (I0) in W/m^2, Angle (θ) in radians

formulas.name: Rayleigh's Criterion
formulas.variables: θ: Minimum angle of resolution, λ: Wavelength of light, D: Diameter of the telescope or microscope
formulas.units: Angle (θ) in radians, Wavelength (λ) in meters, Diameter (D) in meters

formulas.name: Fresnel's Equations
formulas.variables: F1: Reflected amplitude, F2: Transmitted amplitude, n1: Refractive index of the first medium, n2: Refractive index of the second medium
formulas.units: Amplitude (F1, F2) and Refractive indices (n1, n2) are dimensionless

formulas.name: Fresnel's Equations (for normal incidence)
formulas.variables: F1: Reflected amplitude, F2: Transmitted amplitude, n1: Refractive index of the first medium, n2: Refractive index of the second medium
formulas.units: Amplitude (F1, F2) and Refractive indices (n1, n2) are dimensionless

formulas.name: Huygens' Principle
formulas.variables: S: Position of the secondary wavefront, u: Initial position of the primary wavefront, t: Time at which the secondary wavefront is observed, t0: Initial time when the primary wavefront is incident, v: Speed of the wave
formulas.units: Position (S, u) in meters, Time (t, t0) in seconds, Speed (v) in m/s

formulas.name: Diffraction Grating Formula
formulas.variables: d: Distance between slits, θ: Angle of diffraction, n: Order of the spectrum, λ: Wavelength of light
formulas.units: Distance (d) in meters, Angle (θ) in radians, Order (n) is a dimensionless integer, Wavelength (λ) in meters

formulas.name: Young's Double Slit Experiment
formulas.variables: x: Width of the central maximum, λ: Wavelength of light, D: Distance between the slits and the screen, d: Distance between the slits
formulas.units: Width (x) in meters, Wavelength (λ) in meters, Distance (D) in meters, Distance (d) in meters
